channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Author George Saunders shares the experience he wishes he could give to every person
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author George Saunders talks about the experience he wishes he could give to every person.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author George Saunders talks about the experience he wishes he could give to every person.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: Ole Miss hopes to tackle college gambling
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Daniel Durkin, founder of the University of Mississippi's Center for Collegiate Gambling, about gambling among students and the rise of gambling addictions.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Daniel Durkin, founder of the University of Mississippi's Center for Collegiate Gambling, about gambling among students and the rise of gambling addictions.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: Trump's criticism of NATO raises questions of whether — or how — the U.S. could leave
item.description: President Trump has complained that some NATO states aren't letting him use bases in the war against Iran. His criticism has left many wondering if the U.S. may try to withdraw from the alliance.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has complained that some NATO states aren't letting him use bases in the war against Iran. His criticism has left many wondering if the U.S. may try to withdraw from the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Christians in Lebanon try to stay put amid Israel's invasion
item.description: Some Christian villages are besieged as Lebanon's army pulls out of an area where Israel is battling Shia Muslim Hezbollah militants.
item.content:encoded: Some Christian villages are besieged as Lebanon's army pulls out of an area where Israel is battling Shia Muslim Hezbollah militants.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: More and more people experiencing homelessness have been staying at airports
item.description: As homelessness increases in cities around the country, it's also increasing at airports. Some places are upping outreach efforts at airports to get people connected with shelter or services.
item.content:encoded: As homelessness increases in cities around the country, it's also increasing at airports. Some places are upping outreach efforts at airports to get people connected with shelter or services.
item.dc:creator: Erin Bump

item.title: 'Mogging,' rooted in the manosphere, found new life as a joke
item.description: The term "mogging" recently entered the mainstream by way of a viral meme to explain when someone is outperformed. Experts say the phrase is born out of far-right internet forums and warrants examination.
item.content:encoded: The term "mogging" recently entered the mainstream by way of a viral meme to explain when someone is outperformed. Experts say the phrase is born out of far-right internet forums and warrants examination.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to get internet
item.description: As the Israel and the U.S. continue striking Iran, Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey to find an Internet connection with access cut off in Iran
item.content:encoded: As the Israel and the U.S. continue striking Iran, Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey to find an Internet connection with access cut off in Iran
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: How Trump's wartime rhetoric differs from past presidents and why that matters
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with presidential historian Barbara Perry about how President Trump's wartime rhetoric fits into the history of the presidency.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with presidential historian Barbara Perry about how President Trump's wartime rhetoric fits into the history of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Connor Donevan

item.title: For UCLA's Lauren Betts, her biggest battle may have been off the court
item.description: UCLA center Lauren Betts, who may be named player of the year, says while she was battling rivals on the basketball court, a much more serious battle was taking place off it as she dealt with depression and anxiety.
item.content:encoded: UCLA center Lauren Betts, who may be named player of the year, says while she was battling rivals on the basketball court, a much more serious battle was taking place off it as she dealt with depression and anxiety.
item.dc:creator: Steve Futterman

item.title: President Trump's rocky week at home and abroad
item.description: President Trump spent much of the week on the defense after major setbacks ranging from the courts and the economy to foreign policy.
item.content:encoded: President Trump spent much of the week on the defense after major setbacks ranging from the courts and the economy to foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre
